question_text:
The standard reduction potentials at $$298 \mathrm{~K}$$ for the following half cells are given below :
$$\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}+14 \mathrm{H}^{+}+6 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Cr}^{3+}+7 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}, \quad \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=1.33 \mathrm{~V}$$
$$\begin{array}{ll}
\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}(\mathrm{aq})+3 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Fe} & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=-0.04 \mathrm{~V} \\
\mathrm{Ni}^{2+}(\mathrm{aq})+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Ni} & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=-0.25 \mathrm{~V} \\
\mathrm{Ag}^{+}(\mathrm{aq})+\mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Ag} & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=0.80 \mathrm{~V} \\
\mathrm{Au}^{3+}(\mathrm{aq})+3 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow \mathrm{Au} & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=1.40 \mathrm{~V}
\end{array}$$
Consider the given electrochemical reactions,

The mass of the star is $$2.0 \times 10^{32} \mathrm{~kg}$$ and it generates energy at the rate of $$5.808 \times 10^{30} \mathrm{~W}$$. The rate of converting these $${ }^4 \mathrm{He}$$ to $${ }^{12} \mathrm{C}$$ is $$\mathrm{n} \times 10^{42} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$$, where $$\mathrm{n}$$ is _________. [ Take, mass of $${ }^4 \mathrm{He}=4.0026 \mathrm{u}$$, mass of $${ }^{12} \mathrm{C}=12 \mathrm{u}$$]

question_text: A string is wrapped around the rim of a wheel of moment of inertia $$0.40 \mathrm{~kgm}^2$$ and radius $$10 \mathrm{~cm}$$. The wheel is free to rotate about its axis. Initially the wheel is at rest. The string is now pulled by a force of $$40 \mathrm{~N}$$. The angular velocity of the wheel after $$10 \mathrm{~s}$$ is $$x \mathrm{~rad} / \mathrm{s}$$, where $$x$$ is __________.

subject: Physics
micro_topic: Ac Circuits And Power In Ac Circuits
question_text: When a coil is connected across a $$20 \mathrm{~V}$$ dc supply, it draws a current of $$5 \mathrm{~A}$$. When it is connected across $$20 \mathrm{~V}, 50 \mathrm{~Hz}$$ ac supply, it draws a current of $$4 \mathrm{~A}$$. The self inductance of the coil is __________  $$\mathrm{mH}$$. (Take $$\pi=3$$)

subject: Physics
question_text: The current flowing through the $$1 \Omega$$ resistor is $$\frac{n}{10}$$ A. The value of $$n$$ is _______.
